programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: In Bristol, flat-sharing undergraduates Dylan, Ben, Roo and Louis seek advice from Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin on improving their diets. Roo is a Coeliac and Ben a vegetarian, so they shop separately and rarely cook or eat together, and expensive tastes and love of brands mean that they're blowing their student loans on pricey pesto and pasta. Plus, Chris goes to Durham to see what the difference between boxed and bottled wine actually is and dietitian Priya Tew discusses plant-based diets and the potential benefits of eating less meat(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities

programme.title: Countryfile

Matt Baker takes on a challenge laid down by 14-year-old Ethan, who was born with cerebral palsy, while Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown lead the biggest ramble of all along the city of Worcester's riverside. John Craven leads a walk through the grounds of Perthshire's Scone Palace, Anita Rani is on Cornwall's north coast, Adam Henson is in Glenariff Forest Park in Co Antrim, and Tom Heap is in south Wales(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores from the Halloween special taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by Adam Lambert(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Castle Ward on the shores of Strangford Lough in Co Down, where items assessed include a plate from Buckingham Palace, embroidery made from human hair and a traditional Irish wake table. The experts also appraise a Tongan war club that narrowly escaped being chopped up for firewood and a 1960s TV set inspired by the first Moon landing(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: As thousands flee Nazi-occupied Belgium, Harry, Stan and the rest of their unit take the last route out of France - the road to Dunkirk, where Grzegorz fights his way to the front of a queue to board a boat - only to be thrown back by angry soldiers. In Manchester, Douglas finds solace in his rapport with Jan, as his growing anxiety about Tom pushes him to the brink, while in Berlin the Rosslers grow ever more fearful for Hilda. Second World War drama, starring Sean Bean, Helen Hunt and Blake Harrison(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, introducing highlights of Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield, Norwich City v Manchester United at Carrow Road, Arsenal v Crystal Palace at the Emirates Stadium and Newcastle United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at St James' Park. This was the first meeting between the Reds and Spurs since the Champions League final back in June, and Jurgen Klopp will have been hoping for a repeat of his side's 2-0 win to continue their excellent start to the season and maintain their push for a first-ever Premier League title(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of Manchester United v Reading and Arsenal v Manchester City, as well as the rest of the goals from the WSL(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England defender Stuart Pearce, snowboarder Aimee Fuller, featherweight boxing star Josh Warrington and Great Britain sprinter Jeanette Kwakye join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: The team returns with more seasonal stories from around the UK, with John Craven exploring the countryside around the historic city of Oxford and Keeley Donovan heading to Anglesey to find out about the plight of red squirrels(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Dover, Martin Roberts looks around a house in the London suburb of Wood Green, and Martel Maxwell visits a project in Cumbria(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A prolific fraudster comes a cropper after targeting Royal Mail delivery vans, and a careless claimant is caught out when investigators discover his story is full of holes(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A neighbourly dispute gets out of hand, a shop worker fears for his life when two violent men burst in, and a thief goes into a pet shop and steals a puppy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of the barber shop.(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel confides in Zara when she asks his advice about Adam, while Al and Jimmi have an announcement to make, and Ayesha tries to convince a homeless boy to return to his mother(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Somerset for a couple who have a £310,000 budget, and finds out the endangered crane has been brought back from near extinction(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Witley Recycling Centre, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Ollie Allen helping to transform a chair, a wooden chest and a medieval-style tent(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw set off on a search for items around the Trossachs in Stirlingshire, buying a spoon, a stool and a soda syphon along the way(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: Louise Hulland finds out why landlords are at war with tenants over illegal sub-letting and Martyn Sibley reveals why London's biggest car park provider is failing disabled drivers(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel makes Ruby a surprising offer - first refusal to buy the E20 club. But can she persuade Sharon to sell? Keanu pleads with Sharon to stop Louise moving to Portugal, and Mariam confronts Adam about Habiba, leading to an emotional heart-to-heart. Ian gives Iqra and Keegan a dressing down after learning they have been using the restaurant kitchen for their own business(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Drug dealers collect billions of pounds in the UK every year, but how do they get their dirty money into the financial system? Reporter Andy Verity follows the criminal cash from the streets of London to the gold markets of Dubai, revealing how an international crime gang laundered drug money around the world and how bankers and accountants at two big city firms failed to stop it(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Rob realises time is running out on the investigation and determines the key to finding the truth is to unlock Adam's long-lost memories, no matter how painful or traumatic. Meanwhile, Cassie starts to back away from Operation Vestal, spending time with Frank as he schools her in the art of being Lexie as she investigates the dead woman's friends(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Money is getting increasingly tight in the McSweeney household, prompting Conor and Jock to come up with inventive and unsatisfying ways of making food last longer. Determined to help Mairead financially, the lads think about ways they can come up with some extra cash, eventually arriving at an ill-advised plan. Meanwhile, Mairead and Sergeant Healy are in two minds about taking their friendship to the next level. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: BBC Breakfast business presenter Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as comedian and actor Miles Jupp and journalist Camilla Long join regular captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Aquaman star Jason Momoa talks about his part in futuristic TV drama See, Game of Thrones star Emilia Clarke promotes her romantic comedy Last Christmas, while Oscar-winner Regina King discusses her HBO series Watchmen. Top comic Ross Noble also secures a spot on Graham's sofa. Plus, American-Cuban singer-songwriter Camila Cabello performs Liar(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: Marnie desperately wants to get scouted and needs Anton to let her play with the boys(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Wildlife presenter and eco-activist David Attenborough joins Lindsey Russell and Richie Driss and earns his Green Badge(n)

programme.title: Live International Rugby League
programme.desc: Tonga v Great Britain (Kick-off 8.00am). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the opening Test in a four-match tour for the Lions, which takes place at Waikato Stadium in Hamilton, New Zealand and is Great Britain's first game since 2007. Former England head coach Wayne Bennett is in charge of the Lions, and he will be hoping for a repeat of his side's 20-18 victory over Tonga in the 2017 World Cup semi-final. With commentary by Dave Woods and Brian Noble(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles in the arena, including a robot designed by a nine-year-old girl, which comes complete with a huge pink axe. Other entrants include a team of students from Falkirk whose robot is made of wood and has a petrol engine, and a family team with an ingenious creation made up of parts sourced from their local scrapyard(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actresses Jennifer Saunders and Patricia Potter take to the road with experts Philip Serrell and Mark Stacey. They travel through Berkshire, Hampshire and Oxfordshire on a hunt for items to sell at an auction in Greenwich, south London(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher select items to take to auction, including a masonic watch and a sought after commemorative mug by Eric Ravilious. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the home of Britain's first Prime Minister, Robert Walpole(n)

programme.title: Laura (1944)
programme.desc: A woman presumed murdered turns up alive and well, confounding the suspicions of the New York detective working on the case, who thought the killer could have been one of her many socialite friends. Meanwhile, he finds himself irresistibly drawn to her as he tries to establish the victim's real identity. Mystery, with Gene Tierney, Dana Andrews, Clifton Webb and Vincent Price(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in Cardiff - a quirky terraced home, a modern renovated bath-house and a new build that was shortlisted for the Welsh Architecture Prize(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison compete to win a commission to transform a garden room in a three-bedroom Victorian terraced house in Shrewsbury for Mike and Lisa. Robert suggests a structure that gives each of them their own separate spaces, while Laura attempts to blend the needs of the whole family with in a highly adaptable garden room(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers at Calshot on the south coast race to the rescue of a 180-metre-long cargo ship which has run aground on a sandbank, and is now listing at a dangerous angle. At the most northerly lifeboat station on the British mainland, the crew gets called out to investigate a personal locator beacon which has been set off on a remote stretch of rocky coastline, and in Kinsale, volunteers come to the aid of a horse(n)

programme.title: Britain's Classroom Heroes
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Tina Daheley present highlights of the annual Teaching Awards from London's Roundhouse, Those presenting the trophies include broadcaster Joan Bakewell, gymnast Max Whitlock and Girls Aloud's Nicola Roberts(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Capt Mainwaring and the platoon offer to help Pte Godfrey's widowed friend gather in the harvest on her farm - but their well-meaning efforts end in predictable chaos. Comedy first aired in 1972, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Arnold Ridley(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Weasels are often portrayed as villains and associated with bad behaviour. In an attempt to determine whether this assessment is correct, this documentary employs the use of over 50 hidden cameras to follow the adventures of first-time stoat mum Bandita as she raises her kits in a unique weasel wonderland. Cameras capture the creatures interacting, revealing the true nature of the weasel family - feisty and fearless. Narrated by Julie Walters(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures Down Under to explore Australia by train. He begins by riding the legendary Ghan Railway, a train almost a kilometre in length and hauled by two locomotives, which covers nearly 2,000 miles across Australia's red centre. Michael traces its route from the southern harbour of Port Augusta to Darwin on the north coast, along the way meeting the indigenous people of Alice Springs, visiting a kangaroo sanctuary and joining Australians to mark the anniversary of Anzac Day, the First Word War battle that defined their nation(n)

programme.title: Westwood: Punk - Icon - Activist (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary film reflecting on the extraordinary career of fashion designer Vivienne Westwood, looking closely at her artistry, her activism and her cultural significance. Featuring contributions from Westwood herself, as well as Pamela Anderson, Christina Hendricks and Kate Moss(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: Blanca senses that the ballroom scene is on the verge of breaking into the mainstream, and encourages her House of Evangelista children to follow their dreams. Angel pursues her ambition of becoming a fashion model, while Pray Tell joins an activist organisation fighting for the rights of HIV-positive people. Return of the drama, starring Billy Porter(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: Blanca is determined to set a good example to her children and launches a business venture with her life's savings. But being a transgender businesswoman of colour, and from a poor background to boot, means the odds are stacked against her. Meanwhile, Elektra pulls a characteristically bold move, and Ricky has returned to town - and is back with Damon. But has distance made the heart grow fonder?(n)

programme.title: Leviathan (2014)
programme.desc: A mechanic comes under pressure from the Mayor of his small hometown, who wants to own the land his repair shop is built on. In desperation, the mechanic turns to a lawyer for help, unaware that this decision will have devastating repercussions for his life. Drama, starring Aleksey Serebryakov and Elena Lyadova. In Russian(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: After William finds Adso and deduces the meaning of the verses of the Apocalypse on the library frescoes, he begins to suspect that Remigio and Salvatore were followers of Dolcino. When Adso asks for a book about the Dolcinians in the library, he arouses the suspicions and disapproval of Malachia and Benno who warn him to avoid such heretical texts. The papal delegation continues to advance, leaving death and destruction in its wake. John Turturro, Rupert Everett and Damian Hardung star(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Great Chelsea Garden Challenge
programme.desc: Six people are invited to compete in a garden-designing contest, with the winner being given a chance to plot and build a garden on Main Avenue at the Chelsea Flower Show 2015. For their first challenge, the designers have four days and £1,500 to build cottage gardens in the grounds of Harlow Carr, North Yorkshire. They will need to impress RHS judge James Alexander-Smith and the gold medal-winning gardener, Ann-Marie Powell to avoid being sent home. At the end of the competition, one person will win the career-changing opportunity to apply their horticultural flair to a patch on the Main Avenue at the 2015 Chelsea Flower Show. Presented by Joe Swift(n)

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: In November and December, the horticulturist places delicate perennials under shelter, cuts back plants and sweeps up fallen leaves as the first frosts hit the garden. Snowdrops begin to appear as she puts tulip bulbs into the ground, and her thoughts turn to next spring(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Setting out on his horticultural tour, Monty Don finds a tasteful blend of ancient and modern landscaping in the antiquated water gardens of Mexico City, and the post-modern surreal fantasies of Luis Barragan in the Mexican rainforest. On the Caribbean island of Cuba, the intrepid gardener discovers a crumbling urban landscape playing host to a budding natural revolution(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the Forest of Dean, Matt Baker goes on the hunt for the elusive adder, and finds out about a project in the forest that is teaching locals heritage crafts. Margherita Taylor meets the brass band making a 'big noise' in the area. She also explores the extraordinary Puzzlewood to discover how a landscape like this is protected. Adam Henson is on his farm keeping everything crossed as Lexie, his Suffolk Punch mare, gets a pregnancy scan(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Frankenweenie (2012)
programme.desc: A lonely boy uses electricity to bring his dead dog back to life. However, the presence of the animal in the neighbourhood leads to panic over a monster, while the young scientist's classmates are soon also meddling with life and death to revive their own pets. Tim Burton's stop-motion animated fantasy, with the voices of Charlie Tahan and Winona Ryder(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Chicken and Egg
programme.desc: The duo set out on a culinary mission across Europe, the Middle East and America to find and cook the most delicious chicken and egg recipes in the world(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies Michael Buerk on a trip to his childhood home of Hereford, where his culinary memories range from the unpromising tinned spaghetti and overcooked cabbage served up by his grandmother to the wholesome food he found on his girlfriend's family table(n)

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: A look at how predators hunt on the coast, both in the water and on land. The environment is in a constant state of change as the tides ebb and flow, so any opportunities to feed must be taken quickly. The programme features footage of dolphins, octopuses, monkeys, fishing wolves and one of the world's largest ever gatherings of humpback whales(n)

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: The presenter explores Tanzania, Ethiopia, and Somalia. He visits a game reserve where efforts are being made to halt the decline of elephant numbers, explores Addis Ababa with long distance runner Haile Gebrselassie to discover why Ethiopia's economy has gone from strength to strength in recent years, and discovers the impact of war on Somalia, meeting a former NHS doctor who has returned to the country to help rebuild(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Los Angeles Rams v Cincinnati Bengals (Kick-off 5.00pm). Nat Coombs presents coverage of the match from the eighth round of fixtures, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. The Rams - led by quarterback Jared Goff - were the NFC's representative in the Super Bowl last season, but lost the showpiece game to Tom Brady's New England Patriots. The Bengals haven't made it to the play-offs since 2015, and this season looks set to be another disappointing one, with the team losing their opening seven matches. With analysis from Osi Umenyiora, Jason Bell and Mike Carlson(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Peter Jones, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Jenny Campbell consider the profit-making potential of furniture made from old aeroplane parts, a range of organic dog treats, healthy snacks and a company that buys and sells books. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth sets out on its first transatlantic crossing, a four-month deployment on the high seas which has the primary purpose of putting the top secret F-35B Lightning Stealth Fighter to the test. Within hours of leaving Portsmouth, the vessel encounters a Russian warship and needs to take immediate evasive action, and the crew has to deal with floods and dangerous fuel leaks on their way to Florida(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels the length of California. He climbs to the top of one of the world's tallest trees, and meets the fire crews tasked with tackling the ever-present danger of wildfires, a growing threat given the state's changing climate and chronic shortage of water. He also meets street doctors providing much-needed medical support to people living in extreme poverty and visits a city on the desert for Americans who have dropped out of the rat race entirely(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Russell Kane comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo, treating the audience to his own brand of humour and introducing routines by comedy circuit stars Roisin Conaty and Nick Helm(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox helps Welsh shepherd Ioan get a leg up the love ladder, but when he invites three ladies back home, his demanding work threatens to obstruct his shot at romance. Meanwhile, vet George and equine dentist Victoria are both in the grips of a dating conundrum, sheepdog trainer Katy leaves the remote Cumbrian fells to see if romance will blossom with ex-motorbike champ David, and Sara finds out if any of the rural romantics believe they have found everlasting love in the countryside(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Thanks to NASA's Juno mission, scientists are starting to peel back the layers of Jupiter to reveal the wonders within. Professor Kaitlin Kratter reveals how extreme the planet is, with the aid of a quarry and a few rocks, giving some idea of the size of the second largest body in the solar system. And at the National Ignition facility in Northern California, Dr Marius Millot uses powerful lasers normally used for nuclear fusion for an experiment giving an idea of the pressures inside Jupiter. Narrated by Toby Jones(n)

programme.title: The Fall
programme.desc: A mob's punishment of a lone man proves cruel and unusual in this nightmarish short film(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from South Shields, Tyne and Wear, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties are thrown into the mix in York - an eco-friendly futuristic dream home, a converted barn with a modern twist and a five-storey Georgian townhouse(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric agrees to undergo the dangerous brain surgery proposed by neurosurgeon Max. During the procedure, Ric has visions of his dead son and lost love, who present him with a terrible choice between life and death. A pregnant Helen returns to Holby, and Dom tries to support Lofty as he faces the prospect of fatherhood(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin serves up some winning houses for tennis-playing town-seekers keen to test out Shaftesbury as their perfect Dorset town and have £460,000 to make the move. They quickly discover it's a top choice, and not just because it's on a hill but because of a great social scene, high street independents, festival events and stunning green spaces(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers at Calshot on the south coast race to the rescue of a 180-metre-long cargo ship which has run aground on a sandbank, and is now listing at a dangerous angle. At the most northerly lifeboat station on the British mainland, the crew gets called out to investigate a personal locator beacon which has been set off on a remote stretch of rocky coastline, and in Kinsale, volunteers come to the aid of a horse(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Michelin-starred chef Galton Blackiston puts three home cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including making quail's egg wrapped in a nest of potato. Sheree Murphy hosts(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives investigate a man from Margate, who led a life shrouded in mystery. Plus, a Cambridgeshire man who was a legend in tug-of-war(n)

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan follows a family of wild black bears for a year. He arrives in the forests of Minnesota in spring to observe the animals emerging from their dens. However, before he can start filming, he faces the challenge of earning their trust and overcoming his own fear of the creatures, which have a dangerous reputation(n)

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota in the summer to catch up with the black bears, but is upset to discover that cub Hope has been abandoned by her mother. With the young animal facing starvation, he is forced to step in and become a surrogate parent, and balances his new duties with continued study of the rest of the family(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team made up of antiques experts takes on the general knowledge champions, as Raj Bisram, David Harper, Caroline Hawley, Gary Pe and Danny Sebastian hope to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin heads to Exeter to shows former DJ Tricia O'Neall and teacher Phil Briggs the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dev Griffin, Alex Horne, Debbie McGee and June Sarpong test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the latest couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off at the weekend. Plus, interviews with the remaining pairs(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actors David Bradley and Nigel Harman travel to Oxfordshire and Somerset in search of antiques, assisted by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Christina Trevanion. Nigel takes a shine to a domestic appliance of yesteryear, and David comes across an item that resonates with one of the characters he has played(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the final first-round heat as George Orwell fans the Orwellians take on gin drinkers the Junipers. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Mary the Mutant, Krusty the Clown, Chandler Bing, and Scaramanga(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The academic quiz's penultimate first-round match takes place, with four students from Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford pitting their wits against a team from Downing College, Cambridge in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Who Are You Calling Fat?
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nine people who live with obesity move in together to explore what it means to be larger-bodied in Britain today, exploring, examining whether they should love their bodies and embrace their size, or strive to get healthy and lose weight. In the first edition, they meet and settle into a house in the Oxfordshire countryside and begin to share their personal stories. But when Del starts to tell them about the bariatric surgery that he feels has saved his life, Victoria accuses him of having stomach amputation surgery and the battle lines are drawn(n)

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Kevin reluctantly hosts a last-minute Halloween party, while Julia takes the kids trick-or-treating in an attempt to meet a celebrity who has just moved into the neighbourhood. Liz tags along as she has been stood up by a date and Anne is in attendance trying to make sure that the kids are safe - but by the time they reach Kevin's house one of the children is missing(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels the length of California. He climbs to the top of one of the world's tallest trees, and meets the fire crews tasked with tackling the ever-present danger of wildfires, a growing threat given the state's changing climate and chronic shortage of water. He also meets street doctors providing much-needed medical support to people living in extreme poverty and visits a city on the desert for Americans who have dropped out of the rat race entirely(n)

programme.title: Nature's Wonderlands: Islands of Evolution
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Richard Fortey investigates why islands are natural laboratories of evolution and examines the unique and remarkable species that live on them. He begins by heading to Hawaii to investigate how life colonises a newly formed island, and encounters honeycreeper birds whose development rivals that of Darwin's famous finches, as well as carnivorous caterpillars that now cannot eat leaves, and giant silversword plants that thrive in parched volcanic soil at heights of 10,000ft(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Built to shelter ancient China from hostile barbarians to the north, the Great Wall also serves as a cultural curtain, beyond which can be found a variety of traditions. This programme goes outside the Wall to meet tribes of reindeer herders on the Siberian border, and explores deserts to the north where ruined cities were once bustling staging posts for camel trains on the legendary Silk Road(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Police work to uncover the role of Ryan's elder brother Fouad in the money-laundering scheme, and Laure and Gilou make a breakthrough arrest. In French(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: The members of the money-laundering operation close ranks, while Beckriche orders his team to let the fraud squad take the lead. In French(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: Lance is obsessed with finding the thief that stole his gold, which means he can ignore the melodrama going on at home, while Andy's lies are catching up with him too, and the pair decide camping out is the safest option(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: Andy and Lance become eco-warriors when they discover that their beloved oak tree is due to be felled. However, a 24 hour helpline turns out to be more of a hindrance. Later, Andy stumbles upon treasure when, for once, he wasn't even looking for it. Comedy drama, starring Mackenzie Crook and Toby Jones(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: Andy and Lance have one last day on the farm to pull out all the stops and find the gold. Plus, Terry's got a new gazebo, the perfect time for a rally(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell present the edition that first aired on August 25, with Big Country, Jane Wiedlin, Brother Beyond, Bomb the Bass, Womack and Womack, Status Quo, Breathe, Yazz & the Plastic Population and Tanita Tikaram Womack and Womack(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Mark Goodier an edition of the pop chart show first shown on September 1, featuring performances by Level 42, Guns N' Roses, Yello, a-ha, Bomb the Bass and Phil Collins(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The crew of HMS Queen Elizabeth completes the vessel's sea trials, testing an innovative new firefighting system for the first time and marking the 100th anniversary of a plane being successfully landed on a moving ship. The carrier returns to Portsmouth, which will be its home port for the next 50 years, while the pilots of its fighter jets go to the US for additional training(n)

programme.title: Nature's Wonderlands: Islands of Evolution
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Richard Fortey investigates why islands are natural laboratories of evolution and examines the unique and remarkable species that live on them. He begins by heading to Hawaii to investigate how life colonises a newly formed island, and encounters honeycreeper birds whose development rivals that of Darwin's famous finches, as well as carnivorous caterpillars that now cannot eat leaves, and giant silversword plants that thrive in parched volcanic soil at heights of 10,000ft(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Los Angeles Rams v Cincinnati Bengals. Nat Coombs presents further coverage of the match from the eighth round of fixtures, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. With analysis from Osi Umenyiora, Jason Bell and Mike Carlson(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of Manchester United v Reading and Arsenal v Manchester City, as well as the rest of the goals from the WSL(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the hard-working culture that existed in the Clyde shipyards, which were responsible for making the Queen Mary and QE2, despite some of the harshest working conditions in industrial history and dire industrial relations. The programme examines how the employees' work ethic and strength of public support led them to defy the then-government's efforts to close the factories down in 1971 and win the right to carry on shipbuilding(n)

programme.title: The Spy Gone North (2018)
programme.desc: An army major in South Korean military intelligence is assigned a mission to determine whether North Koreans are adhering to their commitment to cease developing nuclear weapons. Spy thriller, starring Jung-min Hwang, Sung-min Lee, Jin-woong Cho and Ji-Hoon Ju. In Korean with English subtitles(n)

programme.title: Border Country: When Ireland Was Divided
programme.desc: Since the border between the UK and Ireland was created in 1922, film crews and journalists have descended there to try and make sense of its absurdities and contradictions, as well as the turmoil it can cause. This programme showcases archival footage from the past 100 years, together with the stories of people whose lives have been affected by the crucial dividing line between the UK and Ireland(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: From Cora's brief fragments of memory, Ambrose finds the remains of a young person buried in the woods, but his steady progress in the case is hampered by the arrival of Captain Anne Farmer of the New York state police(n)

programme.title: Pappano's Classical Voices
programme.desc: Royal Opera House music director Antonio Pappano examines different kinds of opera voices to reveal the qualities of great singers and the physical processes they go through in performance. The first episode focuses on sopranos, featuring clips of classic performances by Maria Callas, Joan Sutherland, Birgit Nilsson, Leontyne Price, and Renata Tebaldi, and contributions from singers including Carolyn Sampson, Diana Damrau, Barbara Hannigan, Anna Netrebko and Eva-Maria Westbroek(n)

programme.title: England's Reformation: Three Books That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: To mark the 500th anniversary of the Reformation, Janina Ramirez tells the story of three books that defined this radical religious revolution in England. Tyndale's New Testament made the word of God accessible to the common man for the first time, The Book of Common Prayer established the Protestant religion, and Foxe's Book of Martyrs enshrined a hatred of Catholicism. Janina discovers how together, this trio of texts had a powerful cumulative effect(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Amber Butchart combines biography, art and the history of fashion to explores the lives of historical figures through the clothes they wore, starting with Charles II(n)

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: An exploration of Australia's underwater ecosystem, one of the natural wonders of the world and the largest living structure on the planet. In the first of three programmes, marine biologist Monty Halls examines the complex form of the coral reef itself and the wildlife that lives on it, from deadly fish-hunting snails and sharks that can walk on land to fighting corals and parrotfish, which cocoon themselves every night(n)

programme.title: Hidden History: The Lost Portraits of Bradford
programme.desc: Shanaz Gulzar sets out to identify and track down some of the people in a collection of portraits that were rescued from a skip outside a small photographic studio in Bradford, west Yorkshire, 30 years ago. Many of the photos depict new arrivals from the Asian subcontinent, eastern Europe and the Caribbean, attracted by the offer of work in wool mills, and together they record the changing face of a British industrial city in the middle of the 20th century(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Jennifer Deschamps investigates the events leading up to bankruptcy of the global financial services firm in 2008, telling the stories of several former employees whose professional lives were ruined when they attempted to raise the alarm(n)

programme.title: The Story of China
programme.desc: Michael Wood tells the story of the Qing, China's last empire, which lasted from 1644 till 1911, doubled the size of the country by including Xinjiang, Mongolia and Tibet, and tolerated a diversity of cultures and religions, including Islam. Michael explores the Qing's heritage with visits to a women's mosque, storytelling houses and a traditional printing house to see how all 48,000 Tang Poems were reproduced using hand-carved wood blocks. He also joins audiences to watch open-air opera in the snow and hears about the first Opium War, when a British expedition destroyed the Qing navy and extracted territory and trading rights(n)

After David James and Nadiya Bychkova were eliminated last week, the 11 remaining celebrity couples take to the dancefloor, hoping to impress the judges and the viewers at home with their Halloween-inspired routines. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the third episode, Vickie and Julie, sisters from Merseyside, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tension rises between Ethan and Rash when Ethan catches Rash making a personal call, but a potential argument between the two is interrupted when a patient starts coughing up blood and goes in to cardiac arrest. Meanwhile, Rosa opens up to David about her past, and Charlie's frustration grows over the lack of support for Duffy(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Aston Villa at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Chelsea at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between City and Villa since March 2016, when two goals from Sergio Aguero helped the Citizens record a comprehensive 4-0 victory. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Everton at the Amex Stadium, Watford v Bournemouth at Vicarage Road, West Ham United v Sheffield United at London Stadium, and Friday night's game between Southampton and Leicester City at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Minnesota Vikings v Washington Redskins at US Bank Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Sapphires (2012)
programme.desc: An Irish music manager discovers four Aboriginal Australian singers in a remote mission in the 1960s, and plans to turn them into their country's answer to the Supremes. The naive girls face a challenging journey to stardom when they are sent to entertain American troops in the Vietnam War. Drama, starring Deborah Mailman and Chris O'Dowd(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.desc: Topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers. Presented by Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nazeem Mungur believes emigrating to the warmer climate of the Southern Hemisphere would improve his health, so he and his family head to Australia's Gold Coast for a week's trial(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents as the hunt for bargains heads to the Brackley Antique Cellar, Northamptonshire, where two teams call upon the services of experts Charlie Ross and Catherine Southon to help them find three items in an attempt to make profit at auction. Christina also takes time out to look at some watches through the ages(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins is in Pembrokeshire, where she visits one of the smallest chapels in Britain and learns about a new statue of St David destined for France's Valley of the Saints(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a campervan-loving couple search for a rural property in the Peak District on a budget of £400,000. Plus, the presenter turns her hand to unique woodcraft(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: A builder who left a customer more than £30,000 out of pocket is back on the programme for the third time, and there is a report on the medical use of cannabis oil. Plus, Lucy Owen meets viewers unhappy with parking cameras in their West Wales village(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Stress levels are running high when rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen face their first lock on the Montgomery Canal. Later, the duo enjoy a spell on a horse-drawn narrowboat, finally experiencing the tranquillity of near silence drifting through a pristine landscape without a roaring boat engine. At a section of canal that is still impassable, they learn of plans to restore the canal back to its former glory(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England defender Stuart Pearce, snowboarder Aimee Fuller, featherweight boxing star Josh Warrington and Great Britain sprinter Jeanette Kwakye join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the fifth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker takes on a challenge laid down by 14-year-old Ethan, who was born with cerebral palsy, while Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown lead the biggest ramble of all along the city of Worcester's riverside. John Craven leads a walk through the grounds of Perthshire's Scone Palace, Anita Rani is on Cornwall's north coast, Adam Henson is in Glenariff Forest Park in Co Antrim, and Tom Heap is in south Wales(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores from the Halloween special taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by Adam Lambert(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Castle Ward on the shores of Strangford Lough in Co Down, where items assessed include a plate from Buckingham Palace, embroidery made from human hair and a traditional Irish wake table. The experts also appraise a Tongan war club that narrowly escaped being chopped up for firewood and a 1960s TV set inspired by the first Moon landing(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: As thousands flee Nazi-occupied Belgium, Harry, Stan and the rest of their unit take the last route out of France - the road to Dunkirk, where Grzegorz fights his way to the front of a queue to board a boat - only to be thrown back by angry soldiers. In Manchester, Douglas finds solace in his rapport with Jan, as his growing anxiety about Tom pushes him to the brink, while in Berlin the Rosslers grow ever more fearful for Hilda. Second World War drama, starring Sean Bean, Helen Hunt and Blake Harrison(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts chair a discussion as an audience in Swansea questions a panel of politicians on the big issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Footballer-turned-property expert Dion Dublin, comedians Bob Mortimer and Lucy Porter, and the lovely Debbie McGee join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of Manchester United v Reading and Arsenal v Manchester City, as well as the rest of the goals from the WSL(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: The team returns with more seasonal stories from around the UK, with John Craven exploring the countryside around the historic city of Oxford and Keeley Donovan heading to Anglesey to find out about the plight of red squirrels(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Dover, Martin Roberts looks around a house in the London suburb of Wood Green, and Martel Maxwell visits a project in Cumbria(n)

programme.desc: A prolific fraudster comes a cropper after targeting Royal Mail delivery vans, and a careless claimant is caught out when investigators discover his story is full of holes(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A neighbourly dispute gets out of hand, a shop worker fears for his life when two violent men burst in, and a thief goes into a pet shop and steals a puppy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of the barber shop.(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel confides in Zara when she asks his advice about Adam, while Al and Jimmi have an announcement to make, and Ayesha tries to convince a homeless boy to return to his mother(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Somerset for a couple who have a £310,000 budget, and finds out the endangered crane has been brought back from near extinction(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Witley Recycling Centre, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Ollie Allen helping to transform a chair, a wooden chest and a medieval-style tent(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw set off on a search for items around the Trossachs in Stirlingshire, buying a spoon, a stool and a soda syphon along the way(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Lucy Owen looks into the cheapest way is to treat a cold, Omar Hamdi meets victims of one of the UK's biggest financial scams, and Rachel Treadaway-Williams hears from unlucky travellers whose holiday did not go according to plan(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel makes Ruby a surprising offer - first refusal to buy the E20 club. But can she persuade Sharon to sell? Keanu pleads with Sharon to stop Louise moving to Portugal, and Mariam confronts Adam about Habiba, leading to an emotional heart-to-heart. Ian gives Iqra and Keegan a dressing down after learning they have been using the restaurant kitchen for their own business(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Drug dealers collect billions of pounds in the UK every year, but how do they get their dirty money into the financial system? Reporter Andy Verity follows the criminal cash from the streets of London to the gold markets of Dubai, revealing how an international crime gang laundered drug money around the world and how bankers and accountants at two big city firms failed to stop it(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Rob realises time is running out on the investigation and determines the key to finding the truth is to unlock Adam's long-lost memories, no matter how painful or traumatic. Meanwhile, Cassie starts to back away from Operation Vestal, spending time with Frank as he schools her in the art of being Lexie as she investigates the dead woman's friends(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: The country is gripped by Banksy fever following the discovery of one of his artworks in Port Talbot, while the staff of Wow Wales! settle into their new premises. Return of the comedy about a tourism agency, starring Sally Phillips(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Money is getting increasingly tight in the McSweeney household, prompting Conor and Jock to come up with inventive and unsatisfying ways of making food last longer. Determined to help Mairead financially, the lads think about ways they can come up with some extra cash, eventually arriving at an ill-advised plan. Meanwhile, Mairead and Sergeant Healy are in two minds about taking their friendship to the next level. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: BBC Breakfast business presenter Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as comedian and actor Miles Jupp and journalist Camilla Long join regular captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Vivek Singh and Marcus Wareing, and guest Sir Lenny Henry, while Jane Parkinson selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Colleagues from a law firm in Aberdeen - Dara, Rachael, Emma and Jonny - compete against each other in a series of baking challenges. programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from Witley Recycling Centre, including a cooker hood and a collection of old jars(n)

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: In Bristol, flat-sharing undergraduates Dylan, Ben, Roo and Louis seek advice from Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin on improving their diets. Roo is a Coeliac and Ben a vegetarian, so they shop separately and rarely cook or eat together, and expensive tastes and love of brands mean that they're blowing their student loans on pricey pesto and pasta. Plus, Chris goes to Durham to see what the difference between boxed and bottled wine actually is and dietitian Priya Tew discusses plant-based diets and the potential benefits of eating less meat(n)

programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, introducing highlights of Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield, Norwich City v Manchester United at Carrow Road, Arsenal v Crystal Palace at the Emirates Stadium and Newcastle United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at St James' Park. This was the first meeting between the Reds and Spurs since the Champions League final back in June, and Jurgen Klopp will have been hoping for a repeat of his side's 2-0 win to continue their excellent start to the season and maintain their push for a first-ever Premier League title(n)

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: With growing numbers of people opting for cosmetic surgery through high-street clinics, what happens when things go wrong? Lucy Adams hears from victims of substandard procedures and asks whether clinics are providing proper aftercare, and highlights concerns about doctors who fly in from abroad to operate then fly out again - sometimes leaving the NHS to repair damage they have caused(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: The documentary follows detectives from the Murder Investigation Team as they hunt down the down the attackers of a 30-year-old man who was beaten up after a night out in Greenwich and died six days later. Croydon CID are on the lookout for a serial burglar who has been caught on CCTV at a number of different spots, while in Westminster, response officers arrest a woman and take her into custody. However, all the cells are full at the nearest station, so they are forced to travel several miles and wait a number of hours before they can check her in(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Drug dealers collect billions of pounds in the UK every year, but how do they get their dirty money into the financial system? Reporter Andy Verity follows the criminal cash from the streets of London to the gold markets of Dubai, revealing how an international crime gang laundered drug money around the world and how bankers and accountants at two big city firms failed to stop it(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Vivek Singh and Marcus Wareing, and guest Sir Lenny Henry, while Jane Parkinson selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Colleagues from a law firm in Aberdeen - Dara, Rachael, Emma and Jonny - compete against each other in a series of baking challenges. Senior solicitor Rachael hopes to impress with her red velvet cake, while Dara aims to win over the judges with his unicorn-themed cake, but will there be too much style over substance?(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker reviews the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester City v Aston Villa and Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: International Rugby League
programme.desc: Tonga v Great Britain. Mark Chapman presents action from the opening Test in a four-match series for the Lions, which took place at Waikato Stadium in Hamilton, New Zealand(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Shropshire to help a man look for a property on a £475,000 budget, and heads to Wenlock to try her hand at the Japanese pot-firing technique of raku(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from Witley Recycling Centre, including a cooker hood and a collection of old jars(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a musicals version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Rufus Hound, Trevor Dion Nicholas, Ria Jones, Sophie Evans, Carley Stenson, John Owen-Jones, Jodie Prenger and Denise Van Outen(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the sixth live episode of this year's series. After David James and Nadiya Bychkova were eliminated last week, the 11 remaining celebrity couples take to the dancefloor, hoping to impress the judges and the viewers at home with their Halloween-inspired routines. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the third episode, Vickie and Julie, sisters from Merseyside, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tension rises between Ethan and Rash when Ethan catches Rash making a personal call, but a potential argument between the two is interrupted when a patient starts coughing up blood and goes in to cardiac arrest. Meanwhile, Rosa opens up to David about her past, and Charlie's frustration grows over the lack of support for Duffy(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Aston Villa at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Chelsea at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between City and Villa since March 2016, when two goals from Sergio Aguero helped the Citizens record a comprehensive 4-0 victory. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Everton at the Amex Stadium, Watford v Bournemouth at Vicarage Road, West Ham United v Sheffield United at London Stadium, and Friday night's game between Southampton and Leicester City at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Minnesota Vikings v Washington Redskins at US Bank Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Sapphires (2012)
programme.desc: An Irish music manager discovers four Aboriginal Australian singers in a remote mission in the 1960s, and plans to turn them into their country's answer to the Supremes. The naive girls face a challenging journey to stardom when they are sent to entertain American troops in the Vietnam War. Drama, starring Deborah Mailman and Chris O'Dowd(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Aston Villa at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Chelsea at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between City and Villa since March 2016, when two goals from Sergio Aguero helped the Citizens record a comprehensive 4-0 victory. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Everton at the Amex Stadium, Watford v Bournemouth at Vicarage Road, West Ham United v Sheffield United at London Stadium, and Friday night's game between Southampton and Leicester City at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers. Presented by Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nazeem Mungur believes emigrating to the warmer climate of the Southern Hemisphere would improve his health, so he and his family head to Australia's Gold Coast for a week's trial(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents as the hunt for bargains heads to the Brackley Antique Cellar, Northamptonshire, where two teams call upon the services of experts Charlie Ross and Catherine Southon to help them find three items in an attempt to make profit at auction. Christina also takes time out to look at some watches through the ages(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins is in Pembrokeshire, where she visits one of the smallest chapels in Britain and learns about a new statue of St David destined for France's Valley of the Saints(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in Dorset assisting two teachers - who have a £550,000 budget - find a country home where they can raise their young daughters(n)

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: In Bristol, flat-sharing undergraduates Dylan, Ben, Roo and Louis seek advice from Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin on improving their diets. Roo is a Coeliac and Ben a vegetarian, so they shop separately and rarely cook or eat together, and expensive tastes and love of brands mean that they're blowing their student loans on pricey pesto and pasta. Plus, Chris goes to Durham to see what the difference between boxed and bottled wine actually is and dietitian Priya Tew discusses plant-based diets and the potential benefits of eating less meat(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a star-studded sports special, as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Kadeena Cox, Perri Shakes-Drayton, Stephen Hendry, Pat Nevin, John Parrott, Lee Pearson, Lawrie Sanchez and Beth Tweddle(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the fifth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker takes on a challenge laid down by 14-year-old Ethan, who was born with cerebral palsy, while Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown lead the biggest ramble of all along the city of Worcester's riverside. John Craven leads a walk through the grounds of Perthshire's Scone Palace, Anita Rani is on Cornwall's north coast, Adam Henson is in Glenariff Forest Park in Co Antrim, and Tom Heap is in south Wales(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores from the Halloween special taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by Adam Lambert(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Castle Ward on the shores of Strangford Lough in Co Down, where items assessed include a plate from Buckingham Palace, embroidery made from human hair and a traditional Irish wake table. The experts also appraise a Tongan war club that narrowly escaped being chopped up for firewood and a 1960s TV set inspired by the first Moon landing(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: As thousands flee Nazi-occupied Belgium, Harry, Stan and the rest of their unit take the last route out of France - the road to Dunkirk, where Grzegorz fights his way to the front of a queue to board a boat - only to be thrown back by angry soldiers. In Manchester, Douglas finds solace in his rapport with Jan, as his growing anxiety about Tom pushes him to the brink, while in Berlin the Rosslers grow ever more fearful for Hilda. Second World War drama, starring Sean Bean, Helen Hunt and Blake Harrison(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, introducing highlights of Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield, Norwich City v Manchester United at Carrow Road, Arsenal v Crystal Palace at the Emirates Stadium and Newcastle United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at St James' Park. This was the first meeting between the Reds and Spurs since the Champions League final back in June, and Jurgen Klopp will have been hoping for a repeat of his side's 2-0 win to continue their excellent start to the season and maintain their push for a first-ever Premier League title(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of Manchester United v Reading and Arsenal v Manchester City, as well as the rest of the goals from the WSL(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England defender Stuart Pearce, snowboarder Aimee Fuller, featherweight boxing star Josh Warrington and Great Britain sprinter Jeanette Kwakye join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: The team returns with more seasonal stories from around the UK, with John Craven exploring the countryside around the historic city of Oxford and Keeley Donovan heading to Anglesey to find out about the plight of red squirrels(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Dover, Martin Roberts looks around a house in the London suburb of Wood Green, and Martel Maxwell visits a project in Cumbria(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A prolific fraudster comes a cropper after targeting Royal Mail delivery vans, and a careless claimant is caught out when investigators discover his story is full of holes(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A neighbourly dispute gets out of hand, a shop worker fears for his life when two violent men burst in, and a thief goes into a pet shop and steals a puppy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of the barber shop.(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel confides in Zara when she asks his advice about Adam, while Al and Jimmi have an announcement to make, and Ayesha tries to convince a homeless boy to return to his mother(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Somerset for a couple who have a £310,000 budget, and finds out the endangered crane has been brought back from near extinction(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Witley Recycling Centre, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Ollie Allen helping to transform a chair, a wooden chest and a medieval-style tent(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw set off on a search for items around the Trossachs in Stirlingshire, buying a spoon, a stool and a soda syphon along the way(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Family Footsteps
programme.desc: Gavin Andrews continues to explore the Smyths' historic connection with Russia when they discover another family member was operating in St Petersburg in the early 20th century. Ralph, the brother of Robert and Lizzie, was ostensibly a businessman but evidence points to him actually being a British spy. The Russian Revolution was to have devastating consequences for this branch of the family and sent them fleeing for their lives(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel makes Ruby a surprising offer - first refusal to buy the E20 club. But can she persuade Sharon to sell? Keanu pleads with Sharon to stop Louise moving to Portugal, and Mariam confronts Adam about Habiba, leading to an emotional heart-to-heart. Ian gives Iqra and Keegan a dressing down after learning they have been using the restaurant kitchen for their own business(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Drug dealers collect billions of pounds in the UK every year, but how do they get their dirty money into the financial system? Reporter Andy Verity follows the criminal cash from the streets of London to the gold markets of Dubai, revealing how an international crime gang laundered drug money around the world and how bankers and accountants at two big city firms failed to stop it(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Rob realises time is running out on the investigation and determines the key to finding the truth is to unlock Adam's long-lost memories, no matter how painful or traumatic. Meanwhile, Cassie starts to back away from Operation Vestal, spending time with Frank as he schools her in the art of being Lexie as she investigates the dead woman's friends(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following a 60-year-old woman who takes on a a record-breaking attempt to row solo across the Atlantic Ocean, which will make her the oldest woman to do so, while back home, her sister is treated for a rare terminal cancer. The programme explores the mental and physical challenges both face(n)

programme.title: Pop Goes Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The music and news stories of 1986, the year that saw a Unionist Day of Action descend into intimidation and street violence and a series of by-elections triggered by Unionist opposition to the previous year's Anglo-Irish Agreement. In June, the Northern Ireland Assembly is dissolved - and police are called in when Assemblymen refuse to leave Stormont(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Money is getting increasingly tight in the McSweeney household, prompting Conor and Jock to come up with inventive and unsatisfying ways of making food last longer. Determined to help Mairead financially, the lads think about ways they can come up with some extra cash, eventually arriving at an ill-advised plan. Meanwhile, Mairead and Sergeant Healy are in two minds about taking their friendship to the next level. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v New Zealand (Kick-off 9.00am). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the opening last-four match, which takes place at International Stadium Yokohama, and will see Eddie Jones' side face Steven Hansen's men. Both sides were impressive in their quarter-final matches a week ago, with England overcoming Australia 40-16 and the All Blacks dominating Ireland 46-14. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Clive Woodward, Jonny Wilkinson and Sean Fitzpatrick, reports from Jill Douglas, Brian O'Driscoll and Maggie Alphonsi, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Lawrence Dallaglio(n)
