programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Jasmine House in Lutterworth, Leicestershire managed by Donna Taylor who believes in a no frills approach. But it doesn't take long for Martin and Sara to realise all the added extras in the room don't add up. During the afternoon Leslie and Chloe show their fighting spirit during a kickboxing lesson and all the B&Bers square up to their host(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the luxury Halliwell House in Cheshire for the third day of the contest, where owners Martin Hulme and Sara Williams charge nearly £500 per night. Donna is astonished at the room rates, while David and Ewa are expecting nothing but perfection, but the self-service approach at breakfast confuses the guests, leaving Martin and Sara defiant in the face of their feedback(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

A massive spread is laid on at breakfast, but when one guest takes issue with the cooked items, Lesley and Chloe worry what impact it will have come payment day(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. David and Ewa Woodward want to challenge Martin's criticism over the way they run their business. Next, Donna Taylor wants to find out more about the issue that caused Martin and Sara to lose sleep. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog-lover Kerry Bolwell-Williams kicks off a week of dinner parties in the Welsh valleys, where she hopes that a full choir and a simple menu will impress her guests and make her an early contender for the £1,000 prize. However, she does not react well when it is suggested that her home has a rather distinctive smell(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Food-lover and theme park fan Dave Morris hosts the second dinner party in the Welsh valleys, hoping his home-grown herbs, hand-picked fruit and complicated menu will win him the £1,000 cash prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Shop owner Tasnim Sardar hosts the third night of the competition in the Welsh valleys, and hopes her Pakistani-inspired party will secure her the £1,000 prize. Financial advisor Gill Leonard likes her curries to be hot and spicy, and is particularly pleased by Tasnim's curried cauliflower(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

He hopes an Ibizan dance lesson and drinks served in his hot tub will help to turn the party around(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in the Welsh valleys, financial advisor Gill Leonard dons the oven gloves to entertain the other contestants, and there is a dramatic start to the evening thanks to a mock fire-breathing dragon. Later, the host makes a remarkable revelation to her shocked guests(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to a man who has had a seizure, but they are warned by the control room that the patient has a history of being abusive toward ambulance crews(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Surgeons must plan their next move carefully when Daisy, a 22-month-old girl, arrives at St George's Hospital by air ambulance after being kicked in the head by a horse at her family's stables. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mike, who has a form of medication-resistant epilepsy, arrives in minors with a bandage around his jaw after falling face-first onto the pavement. Plus, 100-year-old Florence, who was diagnosed with dementia three years earlier, and her daughter Frances arrive at the hospital when the former suffers what appears to be a stroke(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Ninety-one-year-old Cyril is airlifted to hospital after a car crash which required him to be cut from his car. Meanwhile, the medical team treat 57-year-old Caroline, who arrives at St George's with chest pains, plus five-year-old Roman has a pencil stuck in his ear(n)

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priest offends Craggy Island's Chinese community and pulls out all the stops to convince them that he is not racist - but his efforts end in disaster. Sitcom, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon, Frank Kelly and Pauline McLynn, with Ozzie Yue(n)

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas narrates a look back at the decade's TV shows, meeting people who appeared in them, those who watched them and their creators. The first of two programmes focuses on pleasure and leisure on 1970s television, with featured clips including outrageous adult jokes in pre-watershed sitcoms, smoking cigarettes on prime-time panel shows, a surprising history lesson aimed at children, and St Bernard dogs being offered for prizes. The footage is also seen through the eyes of people who missed the decade altogether and who find the moral values exhibited to be a real eye-opener(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: The Baby Boomers' Guide to Growing Old
programme.desc: Seasoned celebrities explore alternative ways to make a living, though journalist Eve Pollard and campaigner Stanley Johnson struggle as assistants at a DIY store(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Surgeons must plan their next move carefully when Daisy, a 22-month-old girl, arrives at St George's Hospital by air ambulance after being kicked in the head by a horse at her family's stables. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mike, who has a form of medication-resistant epilepsy, arrives in minors with a bandage around his jaw after falling face-first onto the pavement. Plus, 100-year-old Florence, who was diagnosed with dementia three years earlier, and her daughter Frances arrive at the hospital when the former suffers what appears to be a stroke(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another chance to see some of the programme's more memorable investigations, including Jimmy Doherty visiting Bulgaria to find out why rose oil is considered liquid gold. programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Becci Moloney hosts the third dinner party in Eastbourne, East Sussex, and prepares a menu of hearty food for her guests. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

However, it's the feedback that leaves the hosts wondering if maybe some of the guests have not been that straightforward(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Talbot in Iwerne Minster, Dorset for the third day of the competition, where hosts Erika Watt and Ellie Gill greet their guests. The rooms do not live up to their good first impression and Callum and Lauren are quick to find fault. At breakfast, tensions simmer as Wendy calls Cameron a creep, and at feedback, some of the comments send Erika and Ellie into fits of giggles(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth visit of the week is to The Old Rectory in Llangattock, south Wales, where young hotel manager Cameron Thomas likes to keep things professional. Despite wanting to find fault, Lauren and Callum are soon to admit their bridal suite is top notch, but Erika and Ellie think there may be some cleaning issues. During an afternoon of canoe racing the group members show their competitive sides(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Unfortunately for him, two of the diners seem more intent on taking verbal swipes at each other than bites out of his food(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre nurse Jenny Fox's dinner party in Devon fails to get off to the best start when Damion Fearnley is not impressed by the dishes on offer and Hector Christie takes exception to her choice of main course. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition explores the challenges that inevitably come with the end of life, and how the emergency services have to learn to cope when dealing with death on a daily basis. In Warrington, paramedics arrive at the home of a man having a suspected cardiac arrest, and with CPR already being carried out and a defibrillator at close hand, the man is given the best possible chance of survival. Plus, ambulance staff and police officers discuss being called to sudden deaths(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: A 12-year-old suffers a broken thigh bone after a motorcycle collision in Oxfordshire, while a paraglider is treated for multiple injuries after a crash on the Cumbrian coast(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedics help a 24-year-old man who is having an epileptic seizure in a supermarket, while an ambulance crew treats an elderly woman whose nose will not stop bleeding(n)

programme.title: The Baby Boomers' Guide to Growing Old
programme.desc: A coach-load of greying, good-time ladies help Roy Hudd cheer up John Prescott as the series takes a wry look at leisure, and seasoned celebrities having the time of their lives(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another chance to see some of the programme's more memorable investigations, including Kate Quilton's investigation into saffron fraud in the Atlas Mountains, Morocco, Mark Tebbutt's exploration of how glace cherries are made and Jimmy Doherty's revelation into what pub grub favourite whitebait actually is(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of previous investigations from the programme delving into the secrets behind mass-produced food. In Romania, Kate Quilton discovers how low-price red wine is made and asks if people can really tell the difference between cheap and expensive varieties. Jimmy Doherty looks into the reasons why so many consumers are avoiding gluten, and Matt Tebbutt finds out which part of the pig goes into chewy sweets(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Pete and Kim Smith cannot decide whether to downsize in Warwickshire or buy a holiday home in the Antequera region of Spain with a view to one day moving there permanently(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Marc and Lucy Champ dream of escaping the rat race for weekends and holidays. However, the pair are torn between east Devon, where Marc grew up, and Comares in Andalucia, Spain. Will it be the weather or the properties that Laura Hamilton and Sara Damergi show them that help make up their minds?(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Copper Beech House in Malvern, Worcestershire, a B&B run by ex-professional rugby union player Steve Lloyd and his partner Kathy Leather(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the pet-friendly Dog in a Doublet in Peterborough, where Della McGinn's passion for animals comes a close second to husband John's passion for food(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Torcroft Boutique B&B in Torquay, south Devon, where Phil and Sharon Hobson hope their added extras will help win over their rivals(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the 600-year-old Rhydspence Inn in Herefordshire, where the medieval character proves charming, although the everyday practicalities prove painful for some(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Copper Beech House are first to host and Steve and Kathy want Phil to swallow his synthetic sausage comment(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This competition takes place in and around Liverpool, where the first to host is 41-year-old taxi business owner Rob, who plans a Scouse themed evening. The guests he is hoping to impress are 23-year-old fitness fanatic Toria, 44-year-old property developer Christine, 40-year-old South African business woman Sam, and 20-year-old student Inzy(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is night two in Liverpool, and the turn of businesswoman Sam to try and impress with her South-African-themed evening, though her food is a bit hit and miss(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gym worker Toria hosts the third party in Liverpool, and hopes a kissing game around the dinner table, along with her flaky flapjacks, will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Undergraduate Inzy is the fourth host in Liverpool, and he is on a mission to prove students really can cook. The flirting between him and Toria continues, but will he allow love to stand in way of the competition?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Liverpool, property developer Christine plans to wow her guests with a Spanish holiday-themed dinner party. However, the mood is rather more feisty than fiesta as on-going tensions finally come to a head and it's not long before the honesty - and the sparks - begin to fly(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go head to head as they meet fed-up families whose homes no longer fit their requirements. After listening to their issues, Phil tries to persuade the owners to sell and upgrade on the property market, while Kirstie argues that investing, either by extending or redesigning, is the wiser move. In the first programme, Paul and Dawn, their three children, and his twin 17-year-old daughters from a previous relationship, find their current home isn't working for them any longer. While Kirstie suggests a complete overhaul, Phil is doing what he does best and finds a selection of alternative properties(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of pioneering veterinary practice Fitzpatrick Referrals in Surrey, where orthopaedic-neuro veterinary surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick and his team help some of the nation's hardest-to-cure pets using cutting-edge procedures. A woman arrives with 14 of her rescue dogs, including a tiny papillon in need of a double knee replacement. Noel and his team also fit a German shepherd with a specially designed bionic foot, and treat a mountain dog that is suffering problems with its spine(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits Thomas and Dervla O'Hare, an ambitious couple who set out to construct a spectacular split-level home cascading down an almost vertical Belfast hillside. The plan was to complete the build quickly, but because they lacked any prior experience, managing the complicated project proved a real challenge(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel expand the project by setting up a luxury campsite on the chateau grounds, but they only have six weeks to complete it before the first guests arrive - and the weather is not on their side, with constant rain turning the planned site into a muddy field. They also have to contend with a visit from Dick's family at Easter(n)

programme.title: A Royal Tour of the 20th Century
programme.desc: Documentary examining official tours and state visits by the royal family, exploring the history of the symbolic locations on the touring map, beginning by capturing the celebration and optimism after the end of the Second World War(n)

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas narrates another look back at TV shows from past decades, with clips and reactions to programmes from people not around to watch them at the time. This edition focuses on 70s-style education, with a look at the lessons conveyed about society and sex, as well as lazy national stereotypes and the casual attitude of TV towards the health and safety of members of the public(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow a 10-year-old who has severed his finger at school and a stonemason who has dropped a slab of marble on his hand. Meanwhile, a 74-year-old partially sighted woman is brought in by her neighbour after breaking her ankle in a fall, and draws the medical staff into stories of her challenging childhood(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May take a look at increasingly popular tall hatchbacks known as crossovers. Having concluded their primary appeal is to caravanners, the duo spend a couple of days in models by Mazda and Volkswagen and try to get a feel for what life is like for the people they believe would buy one. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond drives a pair of exciting Lamborghinis in Italy - the ferocious Aventador Roadster and the limited-edition Sesto Elemento, which carries an eye-watering price tag of around £2million. Two cars are also put through their paces on the test track, with James in a modern re-creation of the classic Porsche 911 and Aerosmith frontman Steven Tyler in the rather less exotic Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Matt LeBlanc, Chris Harris and Rory Reid buy three used luxury limousines from Rolls-Royce, Bentley and Mercedes for the price of the cheapest new car in Britain. The glamorous cars are then put through their paces in a gruelling six-hour endurance challenge at Silverstone. Plus, Countryfile's Matt Baker drops in to the studio(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank face the Georgia Gambler, while Danielle and Robbie may have struck gold(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz unearth a musical treasure at an old general store, and a hoarder's house refuses to give up its secrets(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Two siblings are better than one when it comes to negotiating, which has Mike looking to bundle in order to save his bottom line(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: An exploration of the effects of the eruption of volcanos in the ocean. From these violent beginnings emerge islands and coral reefs that are home to a vast array of wildlife, including giant manta rays and vampire bugs that thrive in the tropical snow, after a process that takes millions of years. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: The struggle for survival on the region's isolated islands, home to flightless parrots, burrowing bats, giant skinks and tree-dwelling kangaroos. The documentary reveals how the arrival of new species can upset the delicate natural balance, with disastrous consequences, and explores the human history of the region. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: Efforts to preserve the marine life of the region, which is home to the world's richest coral reefs and an array of species, including whales, sharks, albatross and tuna. Despite millions of people relying on its fish stocks, the seas have very little protection and face various threats, with international fishing fleets taking a disastrous toll. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates(n)

programme.desc: The story of Hitler's personal secretary Martin Bormann and the efforts to track him down. programme.title: Titanic: Stories from the Deep

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past

programme.title: The Hunt for Hitler's Scientists

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team travel to Jersey, where they search for the origins of Mont Orgueil Castle, an imposing fortress constructed during the reign of King John. Their excavation is complicated by a newer castle that was built on top of the old foundations in Tudor times - and the entire site is located on a rocky outcrop with sides so steep they can only be scaled by mountaineers(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the experts head to Llancaiach Fawr Manor, near Caerphilly, south Wales, to investigate an ancient moat. The team's geophysicists feel the site should provide them with the ideal conditions to determine what the ditch was originally designed to guard - but the project soon becomes one of the most baffling investigations in the programme's history(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The pursuit of former Austrian policeman Franz Stangl, who served as commandant of the Nazi extermination camps Sobibor and Treblinka(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Kansai International Airport upon the largest man-made island in the world, in Osaka Bay, Japan. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlin's Tempelhof Airport and London's Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from south Wales compete to host the best dinner party and walk away with the cash prize of £1,000. South African born Nigel and his Welsh wife Nikki kick off the competition with dishes from Nigel's homeland, while Darren and Simon attempt to woo their guests with a flavour of their first-class lifestyle on day two, before down-to-earth Neil and Carly offer up a taste of their favourite dishes on the final night(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aspiring singer Lek and footballer boyfriend Josh kick off the series of dinner parties in Aberdeen, aiming to impress their guests with an Asian/West Indian fusion menu. Day two sees Scottish couple Ange and Davie try to exceed expectations by serving up an Australian-themed night, but there are strong reactions to the kangaroo curry. One-time ravers Jason and Jennifer host the final evening, hoping their travel-inspired menu and surprising revelations will help them secure the cash prize(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Newlyweds Jen and Callum kick off the dinner party challenge in Aberdeen with an American-themed menu, while Debbie and her husband Shaun opt for Scottish-inspired dishes on the second night. Day three sees Sam and her boyfriend Jamie host, with each of the couples divulging their pet names for one another, before the winner of the £1,000 cash prize is revealed at the end of the night(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In west London, Daniela and Stuart kick off the culinary competition by serving an Italian menu, but they soon discover Manisha, with husband Akshay in tow, is hard to please. Next, young couple Josh and Georgie stake their claim to the £1,000 prize, planning a night of high quality food and a party atmosphere, complete with a private party bus. Night three sees Manisha serve a fusion menu of Indian and Western food, but the guests are more entertained by her wedding photos(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits PJ's Steakhouse in Queens, New York, to provide his expert advice to the owners. They decided to turn a successful bar into a high-end restaurant, but without any previous experience in managing a business they have found themselves seriously in debt. With an over-confident chef and a partner propping up the bar, co-owner Madalyn faces a few difficult decisions(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Florida to help the owners of Fleming, a Danish restaurant in Miami. A husband-and-wife duo bought the business eight years ago in the hope of continuing the place's success, but started losing money rapidly. With a Cuban chef cooking Nordic cuisine and a filthy kitchen, things go from bad to worse until it all comes to a head on relaunch night(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sushi Ko, a Japanese restaurant in California that is losing up to $20,000 a month. Gordon finds owner and chef Akira lacking in confidence and self-esteem as the strain of an empty business is beginning to take its toll on his relationship with this family(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits California, where his job is to help the owner of Lido Di Manhattan. Lisa Marie Hemmat, an ambitious business school graduate, has been running the restaurant for the past five years, but has failed to exert any control over the kitchen and the menu. The chef tries to teach her the basics of cooking and venue management, but trouble brews when her staff threaten to walk out on relaunch night(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Boca Raton in Florida to turn around the fortunes of Italian restaurant Anna Vincenzo's. The owner opened the establishment in 2001 with the help of her father but the business has failed to meet her expectations, and the chef realises that her ruthless style of management is having an adverse influence, not only on her staff but also on her family(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some very famous faces join the series' opinionated viewers in a special episode for this year's Stand Up to Cancer appeal. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the week's biggest news stories and from hard-hitting documentary series to gritty drama, the critics offers sharp, insightful, funny and sometimes emotional critiques of popular and topical TV shows(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Dispatches investigates the extraordinary friendship between Prince Andrew and multimillionaire paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. As the full extent of Epstein's crimes emerge, the Prince stands accused of sleeping with a 17-year-old girl supplied by the investor. While the scandal deepens, reporter Cathy Newman explores Epstein's British connections, including Robert Maxwell's daughter Ghislaine and the Queen's second son(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: The British Tribe Next Door
programme.desc: Scarlett Moffatt and her family are plucked from the UK and transplanted into a replica of their terraced family home - complete with running water, electricity and their possessions around them - slap bang in the middle of a Namibian tribal village. They are to stay in Otjeme village, home to 100 semi-nomadic Himba cattle herders, who choose to live very traditional lives, but are overwhelmed by what their first impressions. The first Himba visitors arrive at the house - including Kandisiko, who quickly strikes up a friendship with Scarlett - and are shocked by the overwhelming amount of clothes and shoes owned by Scarlett compared to their one outfit(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: An Extra Slice

He also visits a cookery class with a healthy twist, and produces a show-stopping summer veg pie packed with plenty of good stuff(n)

But will it be a modern apartment near the sea or a traditional cave house in the countryside that wins their hearts?(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jill and Paul want to move lock, stock and barrel to Brittany in northwest France, and are looking for a rural project property with enough land to start a smallholding and follow their dream of living a self-sufficient lifestyle. With their £135,000 budget in mind, Danni Menzies sets out to find a selection of properties that will hopefully meet with their approval(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps John and Julie from Cheshire search for their very first home together in Southern Alicante in Spain, with a budget of £120,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman shows a range of properties to Marc and Mat, who are looking to buy a holiday retreat on Majorca on a budget of £200,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Mel and Steve want to buy a holiday home in south Queensland, Australia that will become their permanent home once they retire. The property also has to suit their daughter Vikki, who has relocated to Oz and plans to move in. It's a big challenge for Ben Hillman as he sets out to find a property that gives Mel, Steve and Vikki everything they want from their wish-list, on their budget of £220,000(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Florida to help the owners of Fleming, a Danish restaurant in Miami. A husband-and-wife duo bought the business eight years ago in the hope of continuing the place's success, but started losing money rapidly. With a Cuban chef cooking Nordic cuisine and a filthy kitchen, things go from bad to worse until it all comes to a head on relaunch night(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sushi Ko, a Japanese restaurant in California that is losing up to $20,000 a month. Gordon finds owner and chef Akira lacking in confidence and self-esteem as the strain of an empty business is beginning to take its toll on his relationship with this family(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits California, where his job is to help the owner of Lido Di Manhattan. Lisa Marie Hemmat, an ambitious business school graduate, has been running the restaurant for the past five years, but has failed to exert any control over the kitchen and the menu. The chef tries to teach her the basics of cooking and venue management, but trouble brews when her staff threaten to walk out on relaunch night(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Boca Raton in Florida to turn around the fortunes of Italian restaurant Anna Vincenzo's. The owner opened the establishment in 2001 with the help of her father but the business has failed to meet her expectations, and the chef realises that her ruthless style of management is having an adverse influence, not only on her staff but also on her family(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sam's Mediterranean Kabob Room in Monrovia, California, and within minutes of his arrival, is caught in the crossfire of an argument between the owner, his wife and their children, all of whom are feeling the pressure of working seven days a week. Can the chef save the business and mend the warring family's frayed relationships?(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week. The programme captures their instant reactions and lively discussions from the comfort of their own homes(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some very famous faces join the series' opinionated viewers in a special episode for this year's Stand Up to Cancer appeal. programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig present the semi-final of the baking contest, which tests the four remaining bakers' skills with patisserie. There are only three places in next week's final, and this round of challenges will determine who does not make the cut. The signature dish requires a delicate dome and a perfect finish, while for the technical, the bakers must take croquembouche to the next level. Finally, for the showstopper, they must use all their skills to construct an edible display case(n)

programme.desc: As part of Channel 4's annual Stand Up to Cancer campaign, hosts Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker and guests Kathy Burke and Harry Hill help raise awareness for Cancer Research UK. Plus, the usual comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days as the UK continues its rollercoaster ride towards Brexit. The properties include a pioneering home made entirely from cork in Eton, an innovative Derbyshire stone cottage, a bold family home on the South Downs with a vaulted interior, a micro-home built on the site of a double garage in London, and an extraordinary ultra-low-energy house in Buckinghamshire(n)

programme.desc: Documentary about the horrors of the rental market including a landlord who decided to sell his east London flat so he could afford a property with his girlfriend. But when he told his tenant, things quickly turned sour and the man stopped paying rent and refused to leave. Plus, the story of a pub owner in Lincolnshire trying to evict his tenant, having rented it 10 years ago on a 'gentleman's handshake'. Now, however, he is owed a staggering £40,000 in unpaid rent(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords

Plus, the case of a Birmingham landlord who wants rid of his troublesome tenants, who have not paid rent for more than a year(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords

programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette(n)

Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay(n)

programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum(n)

The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.title: Air Ambulance ER
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-wall documentary following the work of Britain's emergency helicopter medical services. It's all hands on deck to save the life of an 11-year-old boy who has been knocked down by a car on the Isle of Wight, resulting in a serious head injury. A tough granny proves remarkably calm about her extreme ankle break, a bit of laughing gas helps take away the pain for a paintballer injured at an activity centre and the clock is ticking when a brewing thunderstorm threatens a callout to a motorcyclist trapped in a ditch(n)

programme.title: Without a Trace
programme.desc: The team is called in to search for a wealthy juror whose disappearance could mean a murderer walks free from court. Although the defendant is initially the prime suspect, Jack soon wonders if the missing man vanished of his own accord. A change in his will and a valuable painting lead the detectives to suspect the person they are looking for is an escaped Nazi war criminal(n)

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: Three teenagers discover what they assume to be a body in an abandoned Second World War bunker, but the team soon realises the person is still alive. programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Angus and the team are forced to protect arch-rival Murdoc, when they discover his former mentor wants him dead. Jack realises he may still have feelings for Riley's mother(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: John Peter invents a cure for tiredness, but it ends up turning people into zombies(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvbar goes in search of a shooting star, but finds it is nothing but a worthless rock. However, Vic works out that it may have very useful magnetic properties(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar's ship runs aground in the fog and a rival crew steals his loot. Vic tries to get the treasure back by convincing the thieves it has been cursed by the god of thunder(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra writes a play about a brave animal and a princess(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: McMeer causes trouble after getting caught in Zigby's giant kite(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Vic tries to scare off the tax collector by tricking him into thinking there are monsters lurking near the village(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar is kidnapped by his arch rival Sven, and Vic has to find a way to make the ship move without a sail(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra builds a factory when McMeer's runs out of coconut cake(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra looks after Bertie, who is having a clumsy day(n)

programme.title: King Alfred and the Anglo-Saxons
programme.desc: Michael Wood explores the role of Alfred the Great's grandson Aethelstan in creating a kingdom of all England. The presenter travels to Devon, Cumbria, Scotland and Rome to reveal why he believes the English monarch was one of the greatest, and tells the tale of the ruler's learning and law-making, including his development of a national coinage and his defeat of the Vikings and Scots in the Anglo-Saxon Great War(n)

programme.title: Operation Amsterdam (1958)
programme.desc: A stiff-upper-lipped British Army officer engages in a spot of larceny to aid the war effort. Ordered to Amsterdam, he joins forces with two cunning Dutchmen and steals a secret cache of diamonds from a bank vault, thus preventing the gems falling into Nazi hands. Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok(n)

programme.title: Doctors In The War Zone
programme.desc: The United Nations estimates almost 200,000 people have been killed since Syria's civil war began, with many hundreds of thousands more injured. Millions have been forced from their homes, but the full human cost of the conflict is little known to the outside world because of the danger Western journalists face in trying to enter areas not controlled by the government. Most doctors have fled from those locations and many hospitals have been destroyed, but this documentary follows British trauma surgeon David Nott as he makes the perilous journey across the border into Syria to work alongside medics who have stayed behind. He carries out life-saving surgery in a facility forced underground by daily bombardment(n)

programme.title: A Kind of Loving (1962)
programme.desc: A factory worker gets his girlfriend pregnant, forcing them to endure years of misery and dashed hopes as they marry and move in with her disapproving, nagging mother. John Schlesinger's drama, starring Alan Bates, June Ritchie and Thora Hird(n)

programme.title: The Boy Who Stole a Million (1960)
programme.desc: A boy robs a bank to pay for repairs on his father's car, and is pursued by the police and criminals after the money. Comedy adventure, starring Virgilio Teixeira and Maurice Reyna(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a second home abroad(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a second home abroad(n)

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events showcasing London's vibrant culture scene(n)

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow embarks on a grime-filled journey through the histories of three of the world's leading modern cities. He begins with London, using CGI footage and re-enactments to experience life in the capital in the 14th century. He spends a night as a medieval muck-raker, and tries on a pair of wooden platform shoes designed to elevate the wearer above the filth that covered the streets(n)

programme.title: Royally Mad
programme.desc: The most ardent American fans of the royal family take a trip round London in the run-up to Prince William's wedding(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: Investigating one of the worst air disasters for two decades, Malaysia Airlines flight MH17 from Amsterdam to Kuala Lumpur, which crashed, killing 298 passengers on July 17, 2014. It occurred four months after the disappearance of Malaysian Airlines flight MH370, and has led to speculation that only a powerful ground-to-air missile could be responsible, but there are eyewitness accounts of other aircrafts seen flying next to MH17 close to impact. This documentary tracks down eye witnesses, and speaks with secret intelligence sources in an attempt to sort fact from fiction(n)

programme.title: The Flowers of War (2011)
programme.desc: An American drifter arrives in Nanjing in 1937, just as Japanese forces invade. Hiding out in a Western-run church, he poses as a priest, but ends up adopting his role and providing shelter for orphans and courtesans as carnage unfolds in the city. Drama, starring Christian Bale and Ni Ni. In Nanjing dialect, English, Japanese, Mandarin and Shanghainese(n)

programme.title: Secrets of a Windmill Girl (1966)
programme.desc: A dancer at a famous Soho burlesque club is killed in a car crash. The establishment receives a visit from the police, who talk to the other dancers to piece together the details of the woman's life and the circumstances that led to her death. Drama, starring Pauline Collins and Martin Jarvis(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: John Quilter cooks 'posh' mushrooms on toast, taxi driver Joey Bone tries out three Italian restaurants, and one of London's few female butchers is interviewed(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: Investigating one of the worst air disasters for two decades, Malaysia Airlines flight MH17 from Amsterdam to Kuala Lumpur, which crashed, killing 298 passengers on July 17, 2014. This documentary tracks down eye witnesses, and speaks with secret intelligence sources in an attempt to sort fact from fiction(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Cabbie Joey Bone road-tests three burger bars, Kerryann Dunlop whips up a mean chicken stir fry, and Tottenham's soul food sisters do their trademark curry goat(n)

programme.title: The Man Who Finally Died (1963)
programme.desc: A London jazz musician receives a mysterious telephone call from Germany that makes him suspect his long-dead father may still be alive. He heads for Bavaria to investigate, but the truth proves far stranger than he could have expected. Mystery, starring Stanley Baker and Peter Cushing(n)

programme.title: Hell is Sold Out (1951)
programme.desc: An author long assumed dead makes a surprise return, only to find books he did not write are being published under his name. He discovers that another writer, claiming to be his widow, is passing off her own creations as his previously unseen work to get them published. Drama, starring Mai Zetterling, Herbert Lom and Richard Attenborough(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Choice (1963)
programme.desc: A married woman finds herself caught in a moral dilemma when children inadvertently cause the death of her diabetic lover. Drama, starring Anthony Steel, Jeanne Moody and Ballard Berkeley(n)

programme.desc: Exploring the districts of Woolwich, Plumstead and Deptford(n)

programme.desc: Andrew White follows a trail across the island of Alderney, and walks along part of Hadrian's Wall in Cumbria(n)

programme.desc: Not sure what to read next? We offer inspiration as we chat to London-based writers, and those who feature the capital in their works(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the stories behind official royal residences, which act as family homes and places to conduct the business of monarchy, and charts the evolution of their architecture. She begins with Buckingham Palace and learns about its links to London sewers, its features including state rooms and a home for camels and elephants, plus how its chandeliers are cleaned(n)

programme.title: Kate and William: Inside the Royal Wedding
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on preparations for Prince William and Kate Middleton's wedding at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011. The programme includes details of the bride's dress and the couple's honeymoon plans, as well as interviews with friends, old acquaintances from school and former royal staff(n)

programme.title: Jayne Mansfield's Car (2012)
programme.desc: An Alabama woman who left her husband for an Englishman dies in 1969, and her will dictates that she wishes to be buried in her home state. The arrangement results in an awkward first encounter for her two husbands and their families. Drama, directed by and starring Billy Bob Thornton. With Robert Duvall, John Hurt and Kevin Bacon(n)

programme.title: Ripper (2016)
programme.desc: A writer obtains a set of knives supposedly used by Jack the Ripper, and brings them with her to a retreat where she and five colleagues are planning to write a horror film. A series of terrifying events occur, and they find themselves stalked by a malevolent spirit that could be the infamous murderer reborn into the living world. Horror, starring Kelby Keenan and Thomas Thoroe(n)

programme.title: Summerfield (1977)
programme.desc: A teacher arrives in a small coastal community to take over as teacher of the local school. As he investigates the mystery of his predecessor's sudden disappearance, he uncovers a connection with a girl who lives on a nearby island who has a rare blood disorder - and the long-buried secrets of the community. Drama, starring Nick Tate and John Waters(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the stories behind official royal residences, which act as family homes and places to conduct the business of monarchy, and charts the evolution of their architecture. She begins with Buckingham Palace and learns about its links to London sewers, its features including state rooms and a home for camels and elephants, plus how its chandeliers are cleaned(n)

programme.title: Run
programme.desc: Recovering heroin addict Richard spends every day struggling to stay off drugs and only one thing spurs him on - the possibility of re-establishing contact with his teenage daughter. Faced with the prospect of never seeing her again, he's torn between returning to his old ways and salvaging the only relationship that matters to him. The third of four dramas telling the stories of seemingly unconnected people facing life-changing decisions, starring Lennie James, Mona Hammond, Darren Raymond and Brad Damon(n)
